cards.name: [P1] NFX - Gigi Levy-Weiss
cards.description:
**Fund:** NFX
**Partner:** Gigi Levy-Weiss
**Email:** gigi@nfx.com
**Fit Score:** 91/100
**Stage:** Pre-seed to Seed
**Check Size:** $500K - $5M
**AUM:** $1B+ (multi-fund platform)
**Tags:** gaming, AI-infrastructure, seed, playtika-founder

**Fit Breakdown:**
- Gaming Native Fund: 20/20
- AI Gaming Thesis: 20/20
- Operational Expertise (Playtika): 18/20
- Recent AI Gaming Thought Leadership: 18/20
- Check Size Flexibility: 15/20

**Key Investments:**
- Playtika (Gigi founded, $4.4B exit to Chinese consortium)
- Plarium (mobile gaming, acquired for $500M+)
- Moon Active (Coin Master, $2.5B valuation)
- Papaya Gaming (mobile gaming)
- Superplay (mobile gaming studio)
- Oriel Bachar (transforming single-player games into multiplayer tournaments)

**Outreach Angle:**
Gigi Levy-Weiss literally built Playtika into a $4.4B gaming company—he understands gaming at scale better than almost any VC. NFX's recent publications 'AI Games Are Coming' and 'The Next Era of Gaming and AI' explicitly call out AI-native game infrastructure as a core investment theme. Gigi's quote 'First, we love games' demonstrates genuine passion, not just financial interest. NFX's Signal platform for founder-network effects aligns with AI infrastructure plays that leverage data network effects.

---

**EMAIL DRAFT:**

To: gigi@nfx.com
Subject: "AI Games Are Coming" → what happens after the content layer

Hi Gigi,

Building Playtika into a $4.4B gaming company gives you a perspective few VCs have: you know what separates demo AI from production AI that actually ships at scale. Your recent publication "AI Games Are Coming" nails the thesis—AI isn't just optimizing existing workflows, it's enabling entirely new game genres that weren't economically viable before.

But there's a gap between the AI content layer and what studios can actually deploy in production. NFX portfolio companies building AI-native games need runtime infrastructure that handles persistent agents, evolving world states, and procedural systems without crushing unit economics. That's exactly what we're building at NEXUS AI.

We're seeing 10x efficiency gains with early partners who are shipping AI features they couldn't build before. Given NFX's conviction on AI games and your Signal platform's focus on founder-network effects, I'd love to show you how NEXUS enables the infrastructure layer for the next generation of portfolio companies.

Worth 15 minutes to explore the fit?

[CALENDLY_LINK]

Best,
Lucas Fulks
Founder, NEXUS AI

---

**Status:** Ready to send
**Due Date:** 2026-03-01

cards.name: [P2] Moonfire Ventures - Mattias Ljungman
cards.description:
**Fund:** Moonfire Ventures
**Partner:** Mattias Ljungman
**Email:** mattias@moonfire.com
**Fit Score:** 88/100
**Stage:** Pre-seed to Seed
**Check Size:** $100K - $2M
**AUM:** $115M Fund II (May 2023)
**Tags:** gaming, AI-infrastructure, AI-native-vc, seed

**Fit Breakdown:**
- AI Native VC Fund: 20/20
- Gaming Sector Focus: 18/20
- Technical AI Expertise: 18/20
- Atomico Pedigree: 17/20
- Check Size Alignment: 15/20

**Key Investments:**
- MokN (gaming infrastructure)
- Filigran (cybersecurity with gaming applications)
- Faculty (AI platform with gaming use cases)
- Multiple stealth gaming AI companies (per portfolio)
- 90+ total investments across gaming, work, security, finance

**Outreach Angle:**
Moonfire is literally an AI-native VC—they built their own LLMs for sourcing and evaluation. Mike Arpaia (Partner) is a computer scientist who emphasizes combining AI and VC. Mattias co-founded Atomico (one of Europe's top VCs), bringing massive platform experience. Their 'golden triangle' framework (access + efficiency + service-quality) perfectly describes AI infrastructure for gaming. Being AI-native themselves means they understand technical infrastructure plays better than traditional VCs.

---

**EMAIL DRAFT:**

To: mattias@moonfire.com
Subject: AI-native VC + MokN → the infrastructure layer gap

Hi Mattias,

Building an AI-native VC that uses its own LLMs for sourcing puts Moonfire in a unique position—you understand technical infrastructure plays better than traditional VCs because you're running one yourself. Your investment in MokN shows you see where gaming infrastructure is heading.

Your "golden triangle" framework (access + efficiency + service-quality) perfectly describes what AI infrastructure needs to deliver for game developers. But there's a gap in the stack that keeps surfacing: the runtime layer for persistent AI agents in multiplayer worlds. Studios can generate AI content, but they can't affordably deploy AI that evolves, learns, and maintains state at production scale.

NEXUS AI bridges that gap. We're the engine that enables dynamic environments and persistent agents without the compute costs that kill margins—cutting AI runtime expenses by 60% while shipping features studios couldn't build before.

With Fund II actively deploying and your AI-native approach to evaluation, I'd love to show you what we're building for the next wave of gaming founders.

15 minutes next week?

[CALENDLY_LINK]

Best,
Lucas Fulks
Founder, NEXUS AI

---

**Status:** Ready to send
**Due Date:** 2026-03-01

- Gaminate (gaming peripherals/D2C)
- inSTREAMLY (gaming influencer platform)
- HiPets (gaming-adjacent marketplace)

**Outreach Angle:**
Smok explicitly lists gaming, AR/VR, and AI as their three primary focus areas. Borys Musielak founded Filmaster (acquired) before becoming a VC—he's an operator who understands founder challenges. Their investment in Exit Plan Games (founded by ex-CD Projekt leads who shipped Witcher 3 and Dying Light 2) demonstrates access to elite gaming talent. The CEE region has become a gaming powerhouse (Cyberpunk 2077, Dying Light, Witcher)—Smok has direct access to this talent pool. They 'respond to every quality email from a founder' per their website.

---

**EMAIL DRAFT:**

To: borys@smok.vc
Subject: Exit Plan Games + CEE talent → the infrastructure opportunity

Hi Borys,

Investing in Exit Plan Games (founded by ex-CD Projekt/Witcher 3 leads) shows Smok understands something the market is still waking up to: Central & Eastern Europe has become a gaming powerhouse, and the talent that shipped Cyberpunk, Dying Light, and The Witcher is now building the next generation of studios.

Your explicit focus on gaming, AR/VR, and AI creates strong alignment with what we're building at NEXUS AI. CEE studios have the technical depth to push AI-native gameplay, but they need infrastructure that makes deploying persistent AI agents economically viable. The region's engineering culture—built on ambitious, technically complex games—positions them perfectly for AI-native experiences.

We're building the runtime layer that enables dynamic AI-powered worlds without crushing unit economics. For CEE studios already thinking in systems and procedural complexity, NEXUS is the infrastructure layer that unlocks what they want to build.

You respond to every quality founder email. Here's mine—worth 15 minutes to explore how NEXUS fits Smok's thesis?

[CALENDLY_LINK]

Best,
Lucas Fulks
Founder, NEXUS AI

---

**Status:** Ready to send
**Due Date:** 2026-03-02

**EMAIL DRAFT:**

To: kevin@remagineventures.com
Subject: Keewano + Fund II during conflict → AI gaming conviction

Hi Kevin,

Raising a $25M Fund II during the conflict shows serious LP conviction in Remagine's thesis. Your investment in Keewano (gaming analytics AI) demonstrates you're not just talking about AI-gaming convergence—you're actively deploying into it.

Rebranding to "Remagine AI Ventures" signals where you're placing bets: founders at the forefront of the AI revolution. The Israeli ecosystem you've built deep relationships with has produced some of the strongest technical AI talent globally—particularly in infrastructure and systems engineering.

NEXUS AI fits that thesis. We're building the runtime infrastructure that enables persistent AI agents in multiplayer worlds—dynamic environments that evolve, learn from player behavior, and maintain coherence at scale. Studios using our platform are cutting AI runtime costs by 60% while shipping features that weren't economically viable before.

Your portfolio companies attracting follow-on from Bessemer, NFX, and a16z validates your picking ability. I'd love to show you how NEXUS enables the next category of AI-native gaming infrastructure.

Worth a conversation?

[CALENDLY_LINK]

Best,
Lucas Fulks
Founder, NEXUS AI

Hi Lasse,

Your research on "AI x Crypto" explicitly calls out how crypto adds value to AI products—and that intersection is exactly where NEXUS AI operates. Gunzilla Games shows 1kx isn't just theorizing; you're actively deploying into AI-powered gaming infrastructure.
